mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index signals supply-side bottlenecks that increase input costs and compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of contraction periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 5.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates resolving supply chain constraints that boost manufacturing output and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01.
